title: Nephrodium atratum
taxonomy: Nephrodium atratum belongs to the family Matoniaceae and order Matoniales. It is a genus of ferns that are native to tropical regions. The classification of Nephrodium atratum is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This classification places Nephrodium atratum within the broader group of vascular plants. As a member of the ancient lineage of ferns, Nephrodium atratum is a significant part of the plant kingdom's evolution.
uses: Nephrodium atratum, also known as the Maidenhair fern, has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. The plant's fronds and roots have been used to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin problems. In some traditional medicine practices, the plant's extracts have been used as a diuretic and to treat digestive issues. The plant's antimicrobial properties have also been studied for its potential use in treating infections. More research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses of Nephrodium atratum and its potential health benefits.

parts: Nephrodium atratum, also known as Attractylodes, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. preparations: Nephrodium atratum, also known as the Black Lady Fern, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional herbal medicine.
